title: run-manifest.v0
description: Per-run manifest written by the eval runner as manifest.json under runs/<suite>/<case>/<arm>/. Deliberately lean for v0: skill version digests, fixture hashes, and resolved model config arrive in later revisions via a schema_version bump, so consumers must switch on schema_version.

schema_version.description: Contract identifier; always the literal string "run-manifest.v0".

run_id.description: Unique id for this single pool exec invocation (the value passed via --run-id).

name.description: Skill under test (frontmatter name). For without-skill arms this is still the skill whose case is being run.

version.description: Skill semver from frontmatter metadata.version. Content digests arrive in a later schema revision.

agent_name.description: The pool exec --agent-name value used for this run.

pool_version.description: Version of the pool CLI that executed the run (e.g. "0.2.172").

command.description: Full command line of the run as an argv array, exactly as the runner invoked it.

exit_code.description: Process exit code of the pool exec subprocess (negative values indicate termination by signal, per Python conventions).

artifacts.description: Map from artifact name to path (relative to the run directory unless absolute). Typical keys: prompt, stdout, stderr, trajectory_atif, validator_result, manifest.

started_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the runner started the pool exec subprocess.

finished_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the subprocess exited.

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock duration of the run in milliseconds.

validator_result.description: The embedded validator-result.v1 object produced by this run's validator.

harness_debt.description: Every fragile dependency this run relied on (hidden flags, --latest trajectory recovery, history scraping). This list is the evidence file for which harness PRs to do next.

kind.description: Stable kebab-case category of the debt (e.g. "trajectory-recovery-latest", "hidden-flag-run-id").

detail.description: Human-readable description of what was relied on and why it is fragile.
